steps.title: Identify the task requirement
steps.content: The phrase 'an example of the way in which' requires showing HOW Ballard's synthesis works in a specific composition, not merely stating that he blends these traditions.

steps.title: Select the answer that demonstrates the blending
steps.content: D names Pan Indian Rhythms and provides both elements: it is “written in a classical form” and “incorporates Ute notched sticks.” This shows the concrete mechanism of blending. Choices A, B, and C make general statements about Ballard's approach without demonstrating the two traditions functioning together in a single work. By specifying both the Western classical and Native musical elements in one composition, choice D provides the concrete example of the blending method the task demands. That matches choice D — D is correct.
